persons.arguments_against: Bowen is a common surname (50 officials), and neither stint shares an edition-year with the biography, so mechanically the strict co-occurrence bonus is absent. The strongest contrary case would be a second Sir G. F. Bowen — but it collapses immediately: both staff-list rows print the full distinctive name 'Sir George Ferguson Bowen' with the honour G.C.M.G., which the bio dates to 1860; the bio states 'governor of Mauritius, 1879-83; of Hong Kong, 1883' and the stints are Governor of Mauritius listed from 1879 and Governor of Hong Kong listed from 1883 — exact office, colony, date, name, and honour agreement on both. There is exactly one governor of a colony at a time, and the two stints chain end-to-end exactly as the printed career does. No counter-hypothesis survives for either stint, and no other biography is in the case to contest them.

evidence_cited.fact: Sir George Ferguson Bowen | Governor and Commander-in-Chief | Civil Establishment | G.C.M.G.
Sir George Ferguson Bowen | Governor | Civil Establishment | G.C.M.G.

persons.would_overturn: Nothing realistic; only the discovery that the Colonial Office List itself misprinted a different governor's name in both colonies, which contemporary records rule out.

notes: Textbook positive: full three-part name, gubernatorial offices, exact colony-date chaining (Mauritius 1879-83 handing off to Hong Kong 1883), and the dated honour G.C.M.G. on every staff-list row. Absence of edition co-occurrence is an artifact of staff-list coverage years versus bio edition years.
